name: Reflection
summary: Single agent generates, critiques itself, and refines — loops until satisfied.
about: One agent iterates through Generate → Critique → Refine up to 3 times. The critic explicitly says 'SATISFIED' when quality is good enough, ending the loop early. Demonstrates self-improvement without external feedback.
example_prompts: Explain quantum entanglement to a curious 12-year-old
Write a one-paragraph pitch for a developer productivity tool
Summarise the CAP theorem using only a food analogy
Trade-offs between microservices and monolithic architecture

generator.description: Drafts or revises based on the original prompt and any critic feedback
generator.system: You are a Generator. On the first pass, produce a solid draft responding to the prompt. On subsequent passes, you will receive critic feedback — revise your draft to address every point raised.

feedback.description: Critic feedback from the previous round (empty on first iteration)

critic.description: Evaluates quality — outputs SATISFIED or specific feedback
critic.system: You are a demanding Critic. Only say SATISFIED (as the first word) if the draft is excellent — well-structured, specific with no vague claims, and would impress a subject-matter expert. In all other cases, list at least two concrete, actionable improvements. Be strict: a merely adequate draft is not good enough.
